intro: Ochyrorchis quartiniana, also known as the Spring gentian, is a species of flowering plant in the gentian family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and slender stem. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Ochyrorchis quartiniana include flavonoids, iridoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used as a flavoring agent and food additive, particularly in the form of its root powder or extracts.
